label: Multiplayer Sync
title: Multiplayer sync
lead: Experimental early automatic multiplayer sync for CUCoreLib
bodyHtml:
Wait, what?

CUCoreLib has a soft compatibility layer for KrokoshaCasualtiesMP. That is, if KrokMP is not installed, nothing extra is loaded.

Custom items or buildings registered through CUCoreLib can be spawned by KrokMP using the same string ID it already sends over the network.

This is experimental and subject to change!


Due to the proposed overhaul of MP in its next major version (4.0.0), sync methods will be vastly different. As such, CUCoreLib's MP support is focused on backfill rather then being feature-complete.

In other words, it is more focused on making sure that your code will work after the update.

Setup for normal content

For normal CUCoreLib registries, everything is automatic. All you need is the content (mod) with stable IDs on every machine.

CUCoreLib registers built-in snapshot modules during startup for items, tiles, buildings, liquids, statuses, moodles, settings, and save.

You do not need to add dedicated multiplayer support for these.

ItemRegistry.Register("conicalFlask", flaskInfo, flaskSprite);
// This will automatically have multiplayer support, alongside most custom fields ^
BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("SporeMine", new CustomBuildingEntityDefinition
{
    Name = "Spore mine",
    Sprite = sporeMineSprite,
    Components = new[] { typeof(SporeMineScript) }
}); // Only custom scripts will not have multiplayer support

Simple request example

A JObject is just a small JSON-style bundle of values. Use it when one side of multiplayer needs to ask the other side a question.

In this example, the client asks the server whether a feature is unlocked. The server sends back one value: unlocked.

using CUCoreLib.Networking;
using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;
using UnityEngine;

private void Awake()
{
    // This runs on the server when a client asks this question.
    MultiplayerApi.RegisterServerHandler("mymod.is-feature-unlocked", request =>
    {
        string featureId = request?.Value<string>("featureId");
        bool unlocked = featureId == "challenge-mode";

        return new JObject
        {
            ["unlocked"] = unlocked
        };
    });
}

private void AskServerIfGlassworksIsUnlocked()
{
    MultiplayerApi.RequestServer(
        "mymod.is-feature-unlocked",
        new JObject
        {
            ["featureId"] = "challenge-mode"
        },
        response =>
        {
            bool unlocked = response?.Value<bool?>("unlocked") ?? false;

            if (unlocked)
            {
                Debug.Log("Challenge mode is unlocked!");
            }
        }
    );
}

RegisterServerHandler answers a question, RequestServer asks the question, and JObject is the note passed between them.

Custom snapshot modules

Use MultiplayerApi.RegisterSyncModule when your mod owns extra multiplayer state, such as discovered markers, machine recipes, per-world unlocks, or cached server decisions. A module has one capture callback and, optionally, one apply callback.

This may look familiar to the Saving API. Snapshots are sent over the network, instead of being written to the disk.

using CUCoreLib.Networking;
using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;

private readonly Dictionary<string, Vector2> markers = new Dictionary<string, Vector2>();

private void Awake()
{
    MultiplayerApi.RegisterSyncModule(
        "glassworks.markers",
        CaptureMarkerSnapshot,
        ApplyMarkerSnapshot
    );
}

private JObject CaptureMarkerSnapshot()
{
    JObject root = new JObject();
    foreach (KeyValuePair<string, Vector2> marker in markers)
    {
        root[marker.Key] = new JObject
        {
            ["x"] = marker.Value.x,
            ["y"] = marker.Value.y
        };
    }

    return root;
}

private void ApplyMarkerSnapshot(JObject snapshot)
{
    if (snapshot == null) return;

    markers.Clear();
    foreach (JProperty property in snapshot.Properties())
    {
        JObject marker = property.Value as JObject;
        if (marker == null) continue;

        markers[property.Name] = new Vector2(
            marker.Value<float?>("x") ?? 0f,
            marker.Value<float?>("y") ?? 0f
        );
    }
}

Messages and requests

Use messages for live actions, and snapshots for durable state. The public surface is CUCoreLib.Networking.MultiplayerApi, so dependent mods do not need to reflect into KrokMP internals.

These APIs may be lacking, apologies. Due to the situation of v4.0.0, some features may be limited for the time being.

APIUse it for
IsAvailableWhether the CUCoreLib bridge found KrokMP and installed its receivers.
IsRunningWhether KrokMP reports an active multiplayer runtime.
IsClient, IsServer, IsHostRole checks before sending server-only or client-only work.
RegisterServerHandlerHandle client-to-server requests/events for your channel.
RegisterClientHandlerHandle server-to-client events for your channel.
SendToServerFire-and-forget client event.
RequestServerClient request with a server response callback.
SendToClientServer event to one client ID.
BroadcastServer event to all clients, optionally including host.
BroadcastSnapshotServer resend of the current full CUCoreLib snapshot.

Request custom player status data

For body-status sync, use KrokMP's clientId to choose which player body to read. A clientId is the multiplayer player ID, not the player's display name.

GetCustomPlayerData returns the saved custom BodyStatus payloads for that player's body. GetCustomPlayerLimbData returns custom LimbStatus payloads for that player's limbs.

// Ask the server for one player's custom body statuses.
MultiplayerApi.RequestCustomPlayerData(clientId, response =>
{
    JArray bodyStatuses = response?["body"] as JArray;
    Debug.Log("Body status payloads: " + (bodyStatuses?.Count ?? 0));
});

// Ask the server for one player's custom limb statuses.
MultiplayerApi.RequestCustomPlayerLimbData(clientId, response =>
{
    JArray limbStatuses = response?["limbs"] as JArray;
    Debug.Log("Limb status payloads: " + (limbStatuses?.Count ?? 0));
});
